Vessel Behaviour Assessment

Roll is the dominant motion — 4× larger than pitch. Classic catamaran response: stiff in pitch, lively in roll.
Roll period ~3.8s is fast. Short period = quick snappy motion that is more uncomfortable than slow rolling. Crew fatigue risk on long survey ops.
Peak roll rates reach ±10°/s. At the wheelhouse (8m above roll axis), lateral acceleration peaks at 0.14g — above comfort threshold.
Heave is modest (±0.45m) — catamaran hull provides good vertical stability in these conditions.
At Bft 3–4 (mild conditions), roll already hits ±6.5°. At Bft 5–6 during winter survey ops, expect 2–3× amplification.
Heading is stable within ±10° — good directional stability, likely DP or autopilot maintaining heading.

Gyro Stabilizer Effect (estimated)

Without gyro (actual)±6.5° peak, ±1.5° RMS
With gyro (50% red.)±3.2° peak, ±0.7° RMS
With gyro (70% red.)±1.9° peak, ±0.4° RMS

Use the Gyro dropdown to visualize the effect in the 3D simulation. 50–70% reduction is typical for a single gyro unit at this sea state.
